name: lightning-module-enhanced
documentation:
# Lightning Module Enhanced

Trying to make pytorch-lightning more *pythonic*, whatever that means.

Docs [here](docs/).

## Installation

```
pip install lightning-module-enhanced
```

Lowest supported python version: 3.10. We have `lightning-module-enhanced==0.34.xxx` that supports python3.8 as well,
but it's not getting updates.

modules.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics
documentation: Init module for metrics

modules.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.core_metric
documentation:
All metrics in LME are a subclass of this class and follow logic describe below. Only epoch results are
relevant, and batch results are somehow accumulated, such that we minimize the risk of getting invalid results from
simply averaging them during training.

For example, instead of accumulating accuracies like this:
    - epoch_accuracy = (batch_accuracy_1 + batch_accuracy_2) / 2
We do:
    - epoch_accuracy = sum([[batch_accuracy_item_b1_1, ..., batch_accuracy_item_b1_n],
                            [batch_accuracy_item_b2_1, ..., batch_accuracy_item_b2_n]]) / (b1_n + b2_n)

Methods logic:
- forward: Takes one batch of (y, gt) and returns the metric result of that batch
- batch_update: Takes the batch result from forward(y, gt) and updates the internal state of the current epoch
- epoch_result: Takes the internal state of the current epoch and returns the epoch result
- epoch_result_reduced: Takes the epoch result from epoch_result() and returns a reduced variant of the epoch metric
  that can be logged by basic loggers (MLFlowLogger or TensorBoardLogger) or None. If None, then it is not logged via
  self.log() in the LME at epoch end
- reset: Resets the internal state for the next epoch

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.core_metric.CoreMetric.epoch_result
documentation: Called at each epoch end from the LME. Takes the internal state and returns the epoch result

documentation: This is called at each epoch end after compute(). It resets the state for the next epoch.

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.core_metric.CoreMetric.mode
documentation: compatibility with ModelCheckpoint

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.core_metric.CoreMetric.epoch_result_reduced
documentation:
Reduces a potentially complex metric (confusion matrix or multi label accuracy) into a single number.
This is used so that other loggers, such as mlflow logger or tensorboard logger can store these without making
any transformation (i.e. mlflow logger will sum a confusion matrix into a single number).
By default, does nothing. Override this if needed.

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.core_metric.CoreMetric.to
documentation: seter of the device. Overwrite this in your metric implementation if you have more tensors
pylint: disable=arguments-differ

documentation: mean iou metric

classes.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.mean_iou.MeanIoU
documentation:
mean iou based on the multi class classification stats during training. Only epoch results, no batch.
Modes:
- 'global' We store a single statistic for all the elements in a batch and compute a single IoU
- 'samplewise' We store the statistics of each element (also inside batches) and compute an IoU for each of them
Depending on the use case, both are valid. For the Dronescapes paper, they used samplewise which may be a gotcha.
pylint: disable=not-callable

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.metrics.multi_class_accuracy.MultiClassAccuracy.epoch_result_reduced
documentation: Returns the pbar-compatible result, as the average of the per-class accuracy

Multi class accuracy. Computes the accuracy of each N classes independently, based on the .argmax() result.
It is implemented as a global metric, so we count scores for each batch and return these final ones at the end of
the epoch.

modules.name: lightning_module_enhanced.loss
documentation: loss functions module for various losses that are general but not supported in other frameworks

functions.name: lightning_module_enhanced.loss.batch_weighted_ce
documentation:
Batch-weighted cross-entropy loss. Uses same inputs as F.ce() but flattens first. Takes care of nans/infs as well.
There should be no way all the weights are zero. At the end, we multiply by the number of classes s.t. the final
number is in the same range as F.ce(). F.ce() can be thought as F.ce(y, gt, tr.ones(C)). Without doing that,
we end up with F.ce(y, gt, tr.ones(C)/C). We also subtract from C the number of classes with 0 values in the batch

functions.name: lightning_module_enhanced.loss.batch_weighted_bce
documentation: Batch-weighted cross-entropy loss. Must have same shape and y must be AFTER sigmoid, so [0:1] range

will have appened at some timestamps and the new one to another timestamp.
TODO: remove old ones or linearly approximate based on epoch_averagae_duration when they would've happen?

At the end of each epoch, for each optimizer (can be >1), append the current LR. Only 1 LR allowed per optim.
Return: A list of list (all lrs for each optim), even if just 1: [[0.01, 0.001] (o1), [0.01, 0.01] (o2)]

Trainer.predict.
Note: "loss" keys is always included here, as it's supposed that any Trainer active run must have a loss function.

modules.name: lightning_module_enhanced.trainable_module
documentation:
TrainableModule is a standalone mixin class used to add the necessary properties to train a model:
    criterion_fn, metrics, optimizer, scheduler & callbacks.

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.trainable_module.TrainableModule.checkpoint_monitors
documentation: A subset of the metrics that are used for model checkpointing

Trainable module abstract class
Defines the necessary and optional attributes required to train a LME.
The necessary attributes are: optimizer & criterion.
The optional attributes are: scheduler, metrics & callbacks.

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.lme.LightningModuleEnhanced.lme_metrics
documentation:
Pass through all the metrics of this batch and call forward. This updates the metric state for this batch
Parameters:
- y the output of the model
- gt the ground truth
- include_loss Whether to include the loss in the returned metrics. This can be useful when using a different
model_algorithm, where we want to compute the loss manually as well.

documentation: Private methods

documentation: copies the loaded checkpoint to the log dir. Optionally: old best ckpt and metrics.csv history if exist

methods.name: lightning_module_enhanced.lme.LightningModuleEnhanced._prefix_from_trainer
documentation: returns the prefix: "" (for training), "val_" for validating or "" for testing as well

Generic pytorch-lightning module for ml models.

Callbacks and metrics are called based on the order described here:
<https://pytorch-lightning.readthedocs.io/en/latest/common/lightning_module.html#hooks>

Takes a {class}`torch.nn.Module` as the underlying model and implements
all the training/validation/testing logic in a unified way.

Attributes:
\- base_model: The base {class}`torch.nn.Module`
\- model_algorithm: Handles the `y, metrics, x, gt = model.algorithm(batch)` part of the model
\- cache_results: Keeps the previous results during training so they can be accessed from various callbacks
\- device: The torch device this module is residing on
\- summary: A summary including parameters, layers and tensor shapes of this module
\- num_parames: The total number of parameters of this module
\- num_trainable_params: The number of trainable parameters of this module
\- has_trainer: Checks if the underlying LightningModule is attached to a pl.Trainer instance
Attributes (inherited from TrainableModuleMixin):
\- optimizer: The optimizer used for training runs
\- scheduler: The oprimizer scheduler used for training runs, as well as the monitored metric
\- criterion_fn: The criterion function used for training runs
\- metrics: The dictionary (Name => CoreMetric) of all implemented metrics for this module
\- callbacks: The list of callbacks for this module
\- metadata_callback: The metadata logger for this run

Args:

: base_model: The base {class}`torch.nn.Module`
pylint: disable=too-many-ancestors, arguments-differ, unused-argument, abstract-method
